title: Helictotrichon polyneurum
taxonomy: Helictotrichon polyneurum belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a genus of tufted grasses native to Europe. The specific classification of Helictotrichon polyneurum is further categorized under the tribe Poeae. Within the Poeae tribe, Helictotrichon polyneurum is placed in the subtribe Airinae. This classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Helictotrichon polyneurum, also known as blue oat grass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Helictotrichon polyneurum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Helictotrichon polyneurum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with some herbal preparations also incorporating the plant's leaves and stems into capsules or ointments.
